selection_signal: assessment-agent idea score (0-10, five dimensions, weighted sum)
rationale: control_dependence: failure -- C06-P24 (the exception-guided debugging framework 'systematically captur[es] runtime exceptions ... and formulat[es] targeted fixes'), C06-P25/P26 (the coder module chooses Aider vs. OpenHands to make the fix, by task complexity), C06-P27 (the five-step debugging cycle 'continues iteratively until successful execution or reaching a predefined iteration threshold'). score -- C06-P14/P15 (the Assessment Agent scores each idea 0-10 across five dimensions, 'combined using a weighted summation to produce an overall score ... aiding in the ranking process'), C06-P19/P20 (the Orchestration Agent times and targets human feedback at 'high-scoring ideas'). observation_result -- C06-P23 (methodology refinement incorporates 'structured critiques', including 'automated assessments and expert human feedback', of the prior methodology), C06-P28/P29 (the adaptive-evolution implementation loop follows each attempt with 'performance assessment and potential refinement', explicitly 'based on empirical results rather than theoretical assumptions alone'). No fixed_or_predefined/not_reported: every reported step is conditioned on an exception, score, or empirical result, and the source states the dependence. selection_signal names these drivers. selection_objective: local_repair -- the exception-guided debugging cycle (C06-P24/P27). performance_improvement -- C06-P14 (contribution/soundness among the scored dimensions), C06-P28/P29 (refinement is driven by performance assessment of empirical results). The former 'exploration' label moves to generation_scope (below): the diversity in C06 is produced inside idea generation/evolution, not pursued as a selection purpose, so no diversity_directed_selection at the pi level; no uncertainty_reduction or hypothesis_model_discrimination is reported (the Assessment Agent optimizes multidimensional quality, not posterior uncertainty or hypothesis discrimination). generation_scope: candidate_diversification -- C06-P11 (the Idea Innovation Agent uses 'a higher temperature setting' that 'encourages the generation of more diverse and creative outputs') and C06-P16 (idea evolution 'prevents high-scoring ideas from being overly similar or derived from the same original concept'), both generation-side diversification of the idea candidates (reinforced by the novelty dimension in C06-P14). candidate_selection_rule: top_k_ranked -- ideas are ranked by the weighted-sum overall score (C06-P15 'aiding in the ranking process') and the high-scoring ideas are the ones pursued and given targeted human feedback (C06-P14/P15/P19/P20). candidate_execution_rule: until_success -- the exception-guided debugging cycle runs 'until successful execution' (C06-P27); until_budget -- the same cycle is bounded by 'reaching a predefined iteration threshold' (C06-P27), and the adaptive-evolution loop proceeds as bounded 'structured iterations' each followed by performance assessment (C06-P28/P29). No objective label appears in either rule. design_selection_rule: fixed_or_standard_design -- the experimental design follows the constructed methodology built on baseline codes ('understanding baseline codes for different research tasks, serving as a foundation for innovation', C06-P10; 'multi-round experiment planning and execution' as a fixed pipeline module, C06-P02) and is refined by performance in an adaptive-evolution loop ('each implementation attempt is followed by performance assessment and potential refinement', C06-P28). The design is a standard, baseline-derived template refined by performance, not selected among competing designs by an information/discrimination/cost criterion; no discrimination-, information-, or cost-directed design selection is reported. formal_epistemic_utility_evidence: not_reported -- the Assessment Agent's 0-10 scoring is a multidimensional quality score, not a Bayesian model-discrimination objective; no proposition states a prior/posterior, likelihood, or EIG/epistemic-utility term used directly in idea, methodology, or experiment selection (corpus-level footnote).
